sources.title: FX AID / FX AID XL / FX AID 1U Illustrated Manual (Bob Borries)
Happy Nerding FX AID 1U product page

name: FX AID 1U
description: Compact 1U stereo effects processor with 32 user-loadable programs from a library of 200+. Programs include delays, reverbs, choruses, phasers, filters, pitch shifters, drum emulations, and more. CV-controllable.

controls.name: Control 1
controls.description: Primary parameter knob. Function is program-dependent (e.g. Delay Time, Rate, Frequency, Tune, Threshold).
controls.detail: Function labeled in program list · CV assignable

controls.name: Control 2
controls.description: Secondary parameter knob. Function is program-dependent (e.g. Feedback, Depth, Resonance, Attack, Chorus).
controls.detail: Function labeled in program list · CV assignable

controls.name: Control 3
controls.description: Tertiary parameter knob. Function is program-dependent (e.g. Tone, xFade, Decay, Semitone Shift, Knee).
controls.detail: Function labeled in program list · CV assignable

controls.name: D/W (Dry/Wet)
controls.description: Dry/wet mix knob. Controls blend between dry input signal and processed effect output.
controls.detail: Full CCW = dry · Full CW = wet

controls.name: Bank Button (left)
controls.description: Selects the active bank (4 banks of 8 programs). Hold 2 seconds to enter preset selection mode.

controls.name: Program Button (right)
controls.description: Advances to the next program within the current bank. Also used to navigate presets.

inputs.name: R (Right)
inputs.description: Right stereo audio input. Normalled to L for mono-to-stereo processing.

inputs.name: CV / D/W
inputs.description: Assignable CV input. By default controls Dry/Wet mix. Can be reassigned to Control 1, 2, 3, sample rate reducer, or preset selection.

behaviors.name: Program Selection
behaviors.description: 4 banks × 8 programs = 32 user-loadable slots. Factory presets include Delay Tape, Delay Ping Pong, Delay HP, Delay LP, Delay Freq. Shift, Delay Pitch Shift, Delay Clock Sync, Delay Comb, Delay Magneto, Delay>Reverb, Delay>Dual Shimmer, Delay Vowel, Filter Vowel, Wave Folder, Sample Rate Reducer, Bit Crusher, Flanger, Phaser Classic 12, Pitch Shifter, Pitch Shifter Dual, Freq. Shift Up/Down, Chorus > Reverb, Reverb Spring, Reverb Plate Classic, Reverb Room Stereo, Reverb Hall Chorus, Reverb Hall Medium, Reverb Black Hole, Reverb Cloud, Gray Hole Light, Reverb Shimmer Input Dual, Reverb Shimmer Dual.

behaviors.name: Preset Storage
behaviors.trigger: hold right button until LEDs off, then left to select
behaviors.description: Store 4 quick presets including current program + Control 1/2/3 + D/W positions. Recall by holding 2 seconds then navigating with right button.

behaviors.name: CV Assignment
behaviors.description: Reassign the CV input to control: Control 1 (LED 1), Control 2 (LED 2), Control 3 (LED 3), Sample Rate Reducer (LED 4), or Preset Selection (LED 1+5). Click through options then hold to confirm.

behaviors.name: Firmware / Bank Loading
behaviors.description: Build custom banks of 32 programs from 200+ available algorithms via fxaid.app. Download wave file, play into CV input to flash firmware. USB-C not available on 1U — audio-wave update only. Line level amplitude around ±1V expected.

patchIdeas.name: CV-controlled reverb decay
patchIdeas.patch: Load Reverb Hall Medium, assign CV to Control 2 (Pre-Delay), LFO sweeps the ambience as it breathes.
steps: Bank/Program buttons: navigate to Reverb Hall Medium (Control 1 = Decay Time, Control 2 = Pre-Delay, Control 3 = Tone).
Hold right (Program) button for 2 seconds — LED 2 should blink. Click until LED 2 (Control 2) is selected, then hold 2 seconds to confirm. CV input now modulates Pre-Delay.
Control 1 (Decay): 2 o'clock — long hall tail.
Control 3 (Tone): 12 o'clock — neutral.
D/W knob: 1 o'clock — mostly wet.
Patch voice/mix Out to FX AID 1U L Input. R is normalled from L for mono-to-stereo.
Patch a slow bipolar LFO (0–5V range, ~0.1Hz) to FX AID 1U CV/D/W Input.
Patch FX AID 1U L Output and R Output to your mixer L/R.
patchIdeas.signalOut: L/R Outputs — stereo hall reverb at line level, tail length fixed by Decay knob; pre-delay swept by LFO.
patchIdeas.hear: A long hall tail whose early-reflection timing breathes with the LFO — the space seems to expand and contract around the source.
patchIdeas.diagram:
FX AID 1U:
* Program: Reverb Hall Medium
* CV assign: Control 2 (Pre-Delay)
* Control 1 (Decay): 2:00 · D/W: 1:00

- Voice (Out) -> FX AID 1U (In L)
- LFO (Out) >> FX AID 1U (CV / D/W)
- FX AID 1U (Out L) -> Mixer (L)
- FX AID 1U (Out R) -> Mixer (R)

patchIdeas.name: Clocked delay
patchIdeas.patch: Delay Clock Sync locks delay time to an external clock via the CV input — no knob tweaking to stay in tempo.
steps: Navigate to Delay Clock Sync (Control 1 = CV / Clock In, Control 2 = Feedback, Control 3 = Clock Divider).
Hold right button 2 seconds. Click until LED 1 (Control 1) blinks, then hold 2 seconds to confirm. CV input now receives the clock.
Control 2 (Feedback): 12 o'clock — 3–4 audible repeats.
Control 3 (Clock Divider): 12 o'clock — 1:1 (adjust for 1/2, 1/4, 1/8 divisions).
D/W: 12 o'clock — equal dry/wet.
Patch source audio to FX AID 1U L Input.
Patch a square/gate clock (0–5V, ≥2.5V peak) to FX AID 1U CV/D/W Input.
Patch L and R Outputs to mixer.
patchIdeas.signalOut: L/R Outputs — source plus tempo-locked echoes. Delay time tracks incoming clock rate.
patchIdeas.hear: Echoes that land exactly on musical divisions. Turn the Divider knob live to jump between whole/half/quarter note feels without losing sync.
patchIdeas.diagram:
FX AID 1U:
* Program: Delay Clock Sync
* CV assign: Control 1 (Clock In)
* Feedback: 12:00 · Divider: 12:00 · D/W: 12:00

- Voice (Out) -> FX AID 1U (In L)
- Clock (Out) c> FX AID 1U (CV / D/W)
- FX AID 1U (Out L) -> Output (L)
- FX AID 1U (Out R) -> Output (R)

patchIdeas.name: Shimmer pad generator
patchIdeas.patch: Reverb Shimmer Input Dual turns any sustained input into an up+down octave shimmer pad.
steps: Navigate to Reverb Shimmer Input Dual (Control 1 = Decay Time, Control 2 = Amount, Control 3 = Up/Down Xfade).
Control 1 (Decay): 3 o'clock — long shimmer tail.
Control 2 (Amount): 2 o'clock — plenty of shifted content.
Control 3 (Up/Down Xfade): 12 o'clock — equal blend of +1oct and -1oct shimmer.
D/W: full CW (100% wet) — no dry signal, pure pad.
Patch a sustained source (slow-attack VCA output, pad, held chord) to FX AID 1U L Input.
Patch L and R Outputs to mixer.
patchIdeas.signalOut: L/R Outputs — slowly-building octave-shifted reverberant pad at line level.
patchIdeas.hear: An ambient cathedral with ghostly octaves rising and falling around the source. Sweep Control 3 to tilt toward bright (up octave) or dark (down octave) shimmer.
patchIdeas.diagram:
FX AID 1U:
* Program: Reverb Shimmer Input Dual
* Decay: 3:00 · Amount: 2:00 · Xfade: 12:00
* D/W: 100% wet

- Pad Source (Out) -> FX AID 1U (In L)
- FX AID 1U (Out L) -> Mixer (L)
- FX AID 1U (Out R) -> Mixer (R)

patchIdeas.name: Pitch-shift runaway cascade
patchIdeas.patch: Delay Pitch Shift with envelope-swept Semitone control — one drum hit becomes a climbing cascade.
steps: Navigate to Delay Pitch Shift (Control 1 = Clock Divide, Control 2 = Feedback, Control 3 = ±Pitch Shift).
Hold right button 2 seconds. Click until LED 3 (Control 3) blinks, then hold 2 seconds to confirm. CV input now modulates Pitch Shift.
Control 2 (Feedback): 3 o'clock — near max, repeats sustain for many iterations.
Control 3 (Pitch Shift): 12 o'clock — set manual shift to zero so the envelope does the work.
D/W: 2 o'clock — mostly wet.
Patch a single percussion/pluck source to FX AID 1U L Input.
Patch a slow ramp envelope (0–5V rising) to FX AID 1U CV/D/W Input — each repeat transposes upward as the ramp climbs.
Patch L and R Outputs to mixer. Trigger the envelope once per cascade.
patchIdeas.signalOut: L/R Outputs — one seed hit followed by a climbing staircase of pitched repeats, Shepard-like, at line level.
patchIdeas.hear: A single percussion strike turns into a tumbling waterfall of ever-higher echoes. Lower Feedback to shorten the cascade, or invert the ramp for a tumbling-downward trail.
patchIdeas.diagram:
FX AID 1U:
* Program: Delay Pitch Shift
* CV assign: Control 3 (±Pitch Shift)
* Feedback: 3:00 · D/W: 2:00

- Percussion (Out) -> FX AID 1U (In L)
- Envelope (Ramp) >> FX AID 1U (CV / D/W) // each repeat transposes up
- FX AID 1U (Out L) -> Output (L)
- FX AID 1U (Out R) -> Output (R)

patchIdeas.name: Self-oscillating LP delay howl
patchIdeas.patch: Delay LP pushed past unity feedback self-oscillates — CV-swept filter frequency turns it into a howling voice.
steps: Navigate to Delay LP (Control 1 = Clock Divide, Control 2 = Feedback, Control 3 = Frequency).
Hold right button 2 seconds. Click until LED 3 (Control 3) blinks, then hold 2 seconds to confirm. CV input now modulates the lowpass Frequency inside the feedback loop.
Control 2 (Feedback): 3 o'clock — past unity, the delay begins to self-oscillate/howl.
Control 1 (Clock Divide): 9 o'clock — short delay time for tight howl.
D/W: full CW — 100% wet.
Patch a short gate (~10ms) to FX AID 1U L Input — brief noise bursts seed the feedback loop.
Patch a sequencer or LFO (0–5V) to FX AID 1U CV/D/W Input — sweeps the filter to change howl pitch.
Patch L and R Outputs to mixer.
patchIdeas.signalOut: L/R Outputs — sustained self-oscillating howl at line level, pitch set by the filter frequency.
patchIdeas.hear: A woozy, sliding howl that follows the sequencer. Not chromatic (no V/Oct tracking), but musical slides and gestures. Drop Feedback to choke the howl instantly.
patchIdeas.diagram:
FX AID 1U:
* Program: Delay LP
* Feedback: 3:00 (past unity) · D/W: 100% wet
* CV assign: Control 3 (Frequency)

- Gate/Click (Out) -> FX AID 1U (In L) // seeds feedback
- Sequencer (CV) >> FX AID 1U (CV / D/W) // sweeps filter
- FX AID 1U (Out L) -> Output (L)
- FX AID 1U (Out R) -> Output (R)

patchIdeas.name: Karplus-Strong pluck
patchIdeas.patch: Delay Karplus-Strong with short noise bursts excites a plucked-string tone. Clock Divide sets string length.
steps: Navigate to Delay Karplus-Strong (Control 1 = Clock Divide, Control 2 = Feedback, Control 3 = Tone).
Control 1 (Clock Divide): start at 10 o'clock — higher strings. Sweep live to change pitch.
Control 2 (Feedback): 3 o'clock — long string decay.
Control 3 (Tone): 12 o'clock — neutral timbre. CCW for mellow, CW for bright.
D/W: full CW — 100% wet, pure string.
Patch a noise source through a VCA. Patch a short trigger envelope (~10ms decay) to the VCA CV.
Patch the VCA Output to FX AID 1U L Input — each trigger delivers a noise click that excites the K-S string.
Patch L and R Outputs to mixer. Trigger the envelope to pluck.
patchIdeas.signalOut: L/R Outputs — pitched plucked-string tone at line level. Decay length set by Feedback, pitch set by Clock Divide.
patchIdeas.hear: Classic Karplus-Strong string pluck — bright and clean when Tone is up, woodier when Tone is down. Not chromatic at the knob — tune Clock Divide by ear or automate via CV for sweeping melodies.
patchIdeas.diagram:
FX AID 1U:
* Program: Delay Karplus-Strong
* Clock Divide: 10:00 · Feedback: 3:00 · Tone: 12:00
* D/W: 100% wet

- Noise (Out) -> VCA (In)
- Envelope (Trig, ~10ms) >> VCA (CV) // short burst = pluck
- VCA (Out) -> FX AID 1U (In L)
- FX AID 1U (Out L) -> Output (L)
- FX AID 1U (Out R) -> Output (R)

patchIdeas.name: No-input mixer feedback loop
patchIdeas.patch: FX AID closes a no-input feedback loop as its own sound source. Two LFOs animate the loop gain and delay time.
steps: Navigate to Delay Tape (or any Delay program). Control 1 = Heads Ratio/Time, Control 2 = Feedback, Control 3 = Tone.
Hold right button 2 seconds. Click until LED 1 (Control 1) blinks, then hold 2 seconds to confirm.
Control 2 (Feedback): 2 o'clock — hot but controlled.
D/W: full CW — 100% wet.
Patch mixer Aux Send to an external VCF Input.
Patch VCF Output to FX AID 1U L Input — no audio source anywhere in the chain.
Patch FX AID 1U L Output back to the mixer Return (closing the loop).
Patch LFO1 (slow) to VCF Cutoff — sets what frequencies can pass through the loop.
Patch LFO2 (slower) to FX AID 1U CV/D/W Input — sweeps delay time.
Patch mixer Main Out to your monitors. Raise the Aux Send level slowly until the loop howls.
patchIdeas.signalOut: Mixer Main Out — pure feedback-generated drones, howls and squeals, no external source.
patchIdeas.hear: The loop is its own instrument. LFO1 sweeps which pitches the loop can resonate at; LFO2 slides the delay time so the drone evolves. Small Aux Send changes cause big character shifts — play the send knob.
patchIdeas.diagram:
FX AID 1U:
* Program: Delay Tape (or any delay)
* CV assign: Control 1 (Heads/Time)
* Feedback: 2:00 · D/W: 100% wet

- Mixer (Aux Send) -> Filter (In)
- Filter (Out) -> FX AID 1U (In L) // no source — pure loop
- FX AID 1U (Out L) -> Mixer (Return) // closes the loop
- LFO1 (Out) >> Filter (Cutoff)
- LFO2 (Out) >> FX AID 1U (CV / D/W)
- Mixer (Main Out) -> Output (In)
